utility_evaluation.REASONING: There are 5 items requested in the required format: health_coverage, property_coverage, travel_coverage, additional_services, total_budget. In the final coverage plan, I see entries for health_coverage, adventure_sports_coverage, travel_coverage, and total_budget. Property_coverage is missing entirely, and additional_services is also missing. Next, check if included items are fully specified with all required details: The format asks each coverage item to include costs. Health_coverage has a monthly cost and included services—meets the requirement for costs. Travel_coverage has a monthly cost and included services—meets the cost requirement. Total_budget includes monthly and annual totals—meets requirement. Property_coverage is absent—fails. Additional_services is absent—fails. Also, the format did not explicitly ask for 'adventure_sports_coverage' as a separate item; while it has a cost, it does not map to any required field and cannot substitute for property_coverage or additional_services. Therefore, 3 out of 5 required items are covered with costs and details.

llm_ratings_evaluation.REASONING: The final coverage plan lists three items: EliteCare Premium Plan (health), an unnamed adventure sports coverage (matches 'Adventure Travel Coverage' add-on), and a generic travel coverage without a named provider. From the ground-truth, 'EliteCare Premium Plan' exists under primary health with rating 6. The adventure sports component at €80/month aligns with 'Adventure Travel Coverage' under travel enhancement services, rated 10. The travel coverage at €110/month corresponds to a premium travel option; among ground-truth, 'EliteTravel Premium Plan' has €110/month with rating 5, matching the cost and premium positioning.
